modelMetadata.name: Jack-Tar Deckhand — Presentation Engineering
modelMetadata.description: AI-First Business Service Architecture for a Claude Code skill suite that generates conference-quality PowerPoint presentations. Services are designed as independent, reusable domains orchestrated by a Deck Conductor agent.

services.name: Presentation Engineering
services.mission: Transform ideas into conference-quality visual presentations by orchestrating content, design, image, and assembly services.

services.name: Content Services
services.mission: Transform briefs and topics into structured narratives, outlines, and speaker-ready text.

services.name: Outline Generation
services.mission: Transform a talk brief into a structured slide outline with per-slide type, headline, body points, narrative beat, and visual direction.

services.name: Speaker Notes
services.mission: Generate timed, transition-cued speaker notes calibrated to talk duration and audience profile.

services.name: Design Services
services.mission: Derive and enforce visual identity: palettes, typography, layout rules, and brand consistency.

services.name: Style Derivation
services.mission: Derive a complete StyleGuide (palette, fonts, spacing, layout templates) from a talk brief and optional brand assets.

services.name: Brand Extraction
services.mission: Extract palette from logo, map industry and tone to fonts, derive accessible contrast pairings from minimal brand input.

services.name: Layout Intelligence
services.mission: Apply machine-checkable layout rules: 12-column grid system, content zones, safe margins, visual hierarchy per slide type.

services.name: Image Services
services.mission: Generate, manipulate, and optimise visual assets from text prompts and data.

services.name: Image Routing & Discovery
services.mission: Entry point for all image generation. Discovers available providers at runtime, routes to best model per asset type and budget, returns generated image.

services.name: Ollama Image Generation
services.mission: Generate raster images via local Ollama models (z-image-turbo, flux2-klein).

services.name: Ollama Icon Generation
services.mission: Generate icon and pictogram images via Ollama with icon-specific prompt wrapping.

services.name: Ollama Pattern Generation
services.mission: Generate seamless textures and repeating patterns via Ollama.

services.name: Ollama Diagram Generation
services.mission: Generate illustrative diagrams via Ollama. Honest about text quality limitations — use for conceptual visuals, not precise technical diagrams.

services.name: Cloud Image Generation
services.mission: Generate images via cloud APIs (OpenAI GPT Image 1.5, Google Imagen 4, FLUX.2 Pro) with provider routing based on availability and asset type.

services.name: Cloud Icon Generation
services.mission: Generate SVG icons via Recraft V4 or raster icons via cloud models. SVG output is preferred for scalability.

services.name: Chart Rendering
services.mission: Generate publication-quality data visualisations via Matplotlib and Seaborn at 300 DPI, styled to the brand palette.

services.name: Image Post-Processing
services.mission: Background removal (rembg), resize, crop, colour correction, and file optimisation (pngquant) for generated images.

services.name: Image Generation Expert
services.mission: Advisory AI Persona for prompt engineering, model-specific translation, image quality scoring, and iteration convergence guidance.

services.name: Assembly & QA Services
services.mission: Compose content and visual assets into finished, validated presentation files.

services.name: PPTX Build
services.mission: Compose outline, style guide, image manifest, and speaker notes into a .pptx file via PptxGenJS.

services.name: Visual QA
services.mission: Run 25 automated anti-pattern checks: contrast validation, margin enforcement, font size compliance, consistency audit, image quality thresholds.

services.name: File Optimisation
services.mission: Image compression, font subsetting, metadata stripping, and file size management for the assembled .pptx.

services.name: Presentation Reviewer
services.mission: Review assembled decks against conference presentation best practices: narrative coherence, visual storytelling, pacing, speaker notes quality, audience appropriateness.

services.name: Deck Conductor
services.mission: Orchestrate the full pipeline from talk brief to finished deck. Owns the DeckContext lifecycle, sequences L1 service invocations, manages budget, and handles the QA correction loop.

aiPersonas.description: Top-level orchestration agent that sequences the presentation engineering pipeline. Receives a talk brief from the Speaker, invokes L1 services in dependency order (Design → Content → Image → Assembly → QA → Review), maintains shared state via the DeckContext file system, and manages cloud API budget.

permitted: Parse and validate TalkBrief input
Generate deck plan (slide count, types, narrative flow)
Invoke any L1 service capability in any order
Create and manage DeckContext state directory (./tmp/deck/)
Invoke provider discovery and adapt routing based on available providers
Track cumulative cloud API spend against budget cap
Re-invoke services when QA or Reviewer identifies issues (max 2 correction cycles)
Report progress, cost, and timing to the Speaker
prohibited: Must not generate content, images, or style decisions directly — always delegates to L1 services
Must not spend above the declared budget cap without Speaker approval
Must not skip the QA step
Must not modify generated images or text — only re-invoke the producing service with corrective instructions
Must not proceed past provider discovery without confirming available capabilities to the Speaker
escalationTriggers: Budget will be exceeded by next generation request
QA finds critical issues after 2 correction cycles
Reviewer identifies structural narrative problems requiring Speaker input on direction
Talk brief is ambiguous or missing required fields

dataSources.dataDescription: All DeckContext artefacts in ./tmp/deck/: StyleGuide, SlideOutline, ImageManifest, SpeakerNotes, ChartManifest, QAReport, PipelineState

aiPersonas.description: Advisory AI Persona consulted by image generation skills for prompt engineering, model-specific prompt translation, quality scoring against a 6-dimension rubric, and iteration convergence guidance. Never generates images directly.

permitted: Advise on prompt construction for any image generation model (Ollama or cloud)
Translate visual_direction prose into model-specific prompts
Score generated images against 6-dimension rubric (composition, colour, clarity, relevance, technical quality, text accuracy)
Recommend iteration strategy (accept, refine prompt, rewrite, try different model)
Advise on model selection given asset type and available providers
Provide negative space and style consistency guidance
prohibited: Must not invoke image generation directly — only advises, the calling skill generates
Must not make routing decisions — the imagegen-bridge routes using the expert's advice
Must not access or modify DeckContext state
Must not communicate with the Speaker directly — all communication goes through the conductor or calling skill
escalationTriggers: Image quality score below threshold after max iterations — recommends fallback to next option in degradation chain
Prompt produces safety filter rejections from cloud API — recommends alternative prompt framing

dataSources.dataDescription: Palette for colour enforcement and brand consistency

aiPersonas.description: Advisory AI Persona that reviews assembled decks against conference presentation best practices. Assesses narrative coherence, visual storytelling, pacing, speaker notes quality, and audience appropriateness. Produces structured review with prioritised recommendations. Never modifies the deck directly.

permitted: Review assembled .pptx against conference presentation best practices
Assess narrative coherence for the stated audience and duration
Assess visual storytelling: slide transitions, image choices, visual rhythm
Assess pacing: slide density, breathing room, interaction beats
Assess speaker notes quality: specific and actionable vs generic filler
Produce structured review with per-slide feedback and overall assessment
Recommend specific improvements with priority (critical, suggested, polish)
prohibited: Must not modify the deck, outline, images, or notes directly
Must not re-invoke any service — only the conductor acts on review feedback
Must not override the Speaker's creative choices — flags concerns but respects stated preferences
Must not duplicate deck-qa's programmatic checks — assumes those have already passed
escalationTriggers: Narrative structure fundamentally incompatible with stated format (e.g., 40 content slides for a 15-min lightning talk)
Visual identity inconsistent in ways programmatic QA cannot detect

dataSources.dataDescription: Finished .pptx file rendered to images for visual review
Original slide plan to check build matches intent
Brand and design parameters for consistency assessment
Speaker notes for pacing and quality review
Original talk brief for alignment checking

humanActors.name: Speaker
humanActors.role: Primary user. Provides the talk brief, makes creative decisions, reviews output, approves budget.

serviceAssociations.description: Initiates the pipeline by providing a TalkBrief
Receives the finished .pptx and review feedback

humanActors.name: Reviewer
humanActors.role: Optional human reviewer. Reviews finished deck for content accuracy, brand compliance, and overall quality before delivery.

serviceAssociations.description: Reviews QAReport and Presentation Review output

systemActors.name: Ollama
systemActors.description: Local AI model server for image generation. Provides z-image-turbo (fast photorealism) and flux2-klein (precision/text). Free, sequential generation only. Requires local installation and sufficient hardware.

serviceAssociations.description: Inference endpoint for raster image generation

systemActors.name: OpenAI API
systemActors.description: GPT Image 1.5 — highest quality general-purpose image generation. Best text rendering, strong prompt adherence. $0.009-$0.133/image depending on quality tier. Requires OPENAI_API_KEY.

systemActors.name: Google Vertex AI
systemActors.description: Imagen 4 and Gemini Flash — cost-effective image generation. Imagen 4 Fast at $0.02/image is the budget workhorse for backgrounds and textures. Requires GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT and service account.

serviceAssociations.description: Vertex AI predict endpoint for image generation

systemActors.name: FAL.ai
systemActors.description: Unified access to 600+ models including FLUX.2 Pro (photorealism), Recraft V4 (SVG icons), and Ideogram 3.0 (typography). Single API key. Recommended aggregation layer. Requires FAL_KEY.

serviceAssociations.description: Aggregated API for FLUX.2 and Ideogram models
Aggregated API for Recraft V4 SVG generation

systemActors.name: Recraft API
systemActors.description: Recraft V4 — the only AI model generating production-quality native SVG. Essential for scalable icons and brand assets. $0.04-$0.30/image. Requires RECRAFT_API_KEY. Also accessible via FAL.ai.

serviceAssociations.description: Direct API for SVG icon generation

systemActors.name: PptxGenJS
systemActors.description: Open-source PPTX generation library (v4.0.1, MIT). The rendering engine for assembling slides. Runs via Node.js in Claude Code. Supports slides, text, images, shapes, tables, charts, masters, speaker notes.

systemActors.name: Matplotlib
systemActors.description: Python charting library for headless data visualisation at 300 DPI. Uses Agg backend (no display server needed). Styled via .mplstyle files for brand consistency.

systemActors.name: Filesystem
systemActors.description: Project-local ./tmp/deck/ directory for DeckContext state persistence. JSON files for contracts, image files for generated assets, .pptx for output. Per CONSTITUTION.md Article 4.6, uses ./tmp/ not /tmp/.

interactions.description: Speaker provides TalkBrief to initiate the pipeline
Conductor requests StyleGuide derivation (first step in pipeline)
Conductor requests outline and speaker notes generation
Conductor requests image generation for each slide via imagegen-bridge
Conductor requests PPTX assembly, then QA, then Presentation Review
Conductor delivers finished .pptx, QAReport, review feedback, and cost report
imagegen-bridge probes Ollama availability at startup
imagegen-bridge checks OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable
imagegen-bridge checks GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT environment variable
imagegen-bridge checks FAL_KEY environment variable
imagegen-bridge checks RECRAFT_API_KEY environment variable
Bridge routes to Ollama generation skills for local assets
Bridge routes to cloud generation skills for high-quality assets
Ollama skills call Ollama REST API for inference
Cloud skills call OpenAI API for GPT Image 1.5 generation
Cloud skills call Google Vertex AI for Imagen 4 generation
Cloud skills call FAL.ai for FLUX.2 and Ideogram generation
Cloud icon skill calls Recraft API for SVG generation
Cloud icon skill calls FAL.ai for Recraft V4 access
Chart renderer calls Matplotlib for headless chart generation
Assembler calls PptxGenJS to generate .pptx file
Generation skills consult the Image Generation Expert for prompt engineering and quality scoring
Cloud generation skills consult the expert for model-specific prompt translation
Bridge consults the expert for model selection advice given available providers
Conductor invokes the Presentation Reviewer after QA passes
QA returns findings to conductor for correction cycle (max 2 iterations)
Reviewer returns structured review to conductor for Speaker decision
